title: <x-occurrence>
breadcrumbs: XHTML Elements for Static Server Pages
text: <x-occurrence> XHTML element that defines the way each occurrence of the specified entity is processed when webgen generates the XHTML document for a static server page. Explain this code <x-occurrence { exclude="0" | "1"} { max="Max"} { start="first" | "current"} { atleastone="0" | "1"} name="EntityName">Contents</x-occurrence> Copy Attributes exclude—determines whether the Contents are generated in the XHTML page sent to the browser. Allowed values are "0" ( Contents are generated) or "1" ( Contents are excluded and not generated) If specified, the exclude specification of the parent <x-entity> or <x-occurrence> is overwritten. (This attribute is for compatibility with server pages created prior to Uniface 9.4.) max—determines the maximum number of occurrences to be displayed, where Max is a positive integer. start—determines the occurrence where the current entity should begin to retrieve data. Allowed values are: "first" —the server page is generated using the first occurrences returned from the database. "current" —the page is generated using the current occurrence as a starting point. atleastone—determines whether at least one occurrence of the current entity appears when the server page is run. Allowed values are: "1" —at least one empty occurrence is displayed so the user can enter a search profile. "0" —the Contents is skipped if there is no current occurrence for that entity. name—specifies the fully-qualified and capitalized name of the entity, for example, MY_ENTITY.MY_MODEL Contents — <x-subst> elements that represent the entity fields. Description When copying or inserting Uniface entities into a static server pages, you can select Recurrence options that determine the max, start, and atleastone attributes of the <x-occurrence> element.. When the webgen ProcScript statement encounters an <x-occurrence> element, it locates the specified entity on the current server page. If there is no current occurrence for that entity, the Contents are skipped, unless AtLeastOne="1" is specified. In this case, a single occurrence is included which can be used to enter profile data. Otherwise, the Contents are processed once for each occurrence in the form. A setocc is performed between two iteration steps to make the next occurrence current. When the iteration has been processed completely, the occurrence that was originally current becomes current again. In other words, the original active path is restored. Table 1. Effect of startand max Attributes on Number of Occurrences Displayed start max Occurrences Displayed current absent From the current occurrence to the last occurrence current = n n occurrences, starting at the current occurrence ="first" absent All occurrences ="first" = n First n occurrences The <x-occurrence> element does not appear in the generated XHTML page. Related Links Static Server Pages Static Server Page (USP) components generate a complete web page every time they are activated. This makes them useful for relatively static web pages or a thin-client architecture in which all processing is done on the server. They can also be used for RESTful applications or file upload functionality. Insert Uniface Objects in the Server Page Layout When creating the layout, elements in the layout must be bound to the component data structure as defined in the Component Editor. You can do this by copying the Uniface objects from the component structure and inserting them as HTML in the layout. webgen Generate an XHTML document from the layout definition and the component definition of a static server page. Layout of Static Server Pages Static server page layouts are XHTML-based and include Uniface-specific tags to define the component data structure, as well as standard XHTML for defining the layout and appearance. The structure of the layout is best viewed in the XHTML source. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
